notes: plant on site today: 13 tonne excavator the cat 313, the 3 tonne cat vibe roller, and the bobcat S70 skid steer. all three started at 7am
the excavator did the bulk excavation in zone D from 7 to 11. Steve operating. moved about 200 cubic metres of clay. running well no issues
after lunch Steve switched the bucket on the excavator to the 450mm trenching bucket for the stormwater trench. took about 20 mins to swap over
excavator was trenching from 12:30 to 3:30. about 60 lineal metres of trench at 600mm deep. good progress
total excavator hours today: 7.5 hours operating time. fuel was about three quarters at start of day, down to just under half now. will need a diesel top up tomorrow
the roller was compacting the fill in zone C. Dazza operating from 7am to 2pm. 7 hours on the roller today. did 4 passes over the whole area like the geotech said
roller broke down at about 2pm. the vibration mechanism stopped working mid pass. Dazza says it feels like the belt. called the hire company and theyve got a mechanic coming first thing tomorrow
so the roller is parked up near the site shed. out of action till the mechanic looks at it. hope its just the belt cos if its the exciter bearing were looking at a few days without it
the bobcat was on all day, loading spoil from the excavation into trucks. Bluey operating. ran from 7am to 3:30pm so thats 8.5 hours
bobcat needs new tyres soon by the way. front rights are pretty bald. mentioned it to the boss, he said end of the week at the latest

we had 4 tip trucks running spoil to the dump today. all subbied out to Martins Haulage. each truck doing about 5 loads, 10 cubic metres per load so roughly 200 cubes carted off site total. matches what the excavator dug which is good
concrete pump came on site at 6am for the zone B pour, 42 metre boom pump from Schwing. set up took about 45 mins. pumped for 3 hours straight then packed up by 10:30. total on site about 4.5 hours
the pump had a minor blockage around 8:15, took 10 mins to clear. operator said the mix was a bit dry on that load. second truck was better
tower crane log: 14 lifts today. mostly precast panels and reo cages. no downtime, Johnno kept it moving all day. crane operated from 7am to 3pm, 8 hours
the generator has been running 24/7 for the curing lights on level 2. checked the fuel, topped it up with 40 litres of diesel. should last another 2 days

scaffold: the scaffolders brought a 10 tonne truck with all their gear. erected 6 bays on the east elevation today, about 18 metres long and 12 metres high. took 4 blokes 7 hours. theyll be back tomorrow to do the returns on the north and south ends

the compressor for the nail guns was playing up this morning, kept cutting out. turned out the air filter was clogged with dust. cleaned it out and its been fine since. need to add compressor filter to the weekly maintenance checklist
hired a 7kva generator from Coates for the electricians working in zone E where theres no permanent power yet. picked it up this morning, $95 a day. should need it for the rest of the week
forklift was on site for the material deliveries. used it from 7 to 9am unloading trucks then again from 1 to 2pm for the afternoon delivery. total about 3 hours use today
